goal: Make /app/chat read like a private relationship reply workspace for reviewing one conversation and staging the next response, while preserving all local, side-effect-free behavior.

success_criteria.description: GET /app/chat first viewport leads with one current reply priority: who the conversation is with, why it matters now, the consent/privacy posture, the suggested reply intent, and the next safe action before broader conversation inventory or signal panels.

Conversation, thread, writing assist, summary, extraction, and privacy panels are grouped into a readable reply-review workflow with participant labels, relationship context, consent status, private-note handling, and review status rather than technical inventory.

GET /app/chat?action=record-local-reply shows a usable local reply preview that confirms the selected conversation, draft message, follow-up tracker, and exactly what remains local; reloading the same action route remains stable.

The page and action preview explicitly show that no external message, notification, profile update, analysis of private notes, automated writing call, saved-record write, or outside network request occurs; raw evidence IDs are not dominant primary copy.

GET /app/chat?scenario=empty, pending, failure keep meaningful recovery controls; page tests assert the reply workflow, privacy and consent boundary, action safety ledger, route recovery, and copy guardrails; npm test and npm run build pass with no feature, service, API, fixture, shared UI, shell, contact, event, follow-up, dashboard, agent, profile, or home route edits.

out_of_scope: Changing feature contracts, services, service factories, API envelopes, route handlers, or sample data fixtures
Changing global app shell, global CSS, shared UI primitives, contacts, events, follow-ups, profile, dashboard, agent, or home routes
Adding live chat transport, email, calendar, CRM, notification, messaging, storage, search, AI, analytics, or network integrations
Changing conversation, privacy, writing assist, summary, or extraction feature behavior outside the route composition layer
